---
title: "UI_R019: AI Process Notification Rule"
subtitle: "All AI/OpenAI API processes expected to run longer than 2 seconds must provide real-time stage-based notifications to prevent user anxiety and communicate system status"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "rule"
number: "UI_R019"
date-created: "2025-11-02"
date-modified: "2025-11-02"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "UI Components Law"
article-number:
  number: 19
  title: "AI Process Notification Rule"
related_to:
  - "MP099: Real-Time Progress Reporting"
  - "UI_R007: UI Text Standardization Rule"
  - "MP052: Unidirectional Data Flow"
  - "UI_R001: UI-Server-Defaults Triple Rule"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 19: AI Process Notification Rule {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 19 (AI Process Notification Rule)**

All AI/OpenAI API processes expected to run longer than 2 seconds must provide real-time stage-based notifications to prevent user anxiety and communicate system status effectively.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Requirement
Any component using AI/OpenAI API calls, async processing, or multi-stage data analysis must implement progressive notification to:

1. **Prevent "Broken System" Perception**: Static messages for > 10 seconds make users think system is frozen
2. **Reduce Perceived Wait Time**: Progressive feedback psychologically reduces waiting experience
3. **Build User Trust**: Transparent process stages demonstrate system is actively working
4. **Enable Informed Decisions**: Users can assess whether to wait or continue other work
:::

# Section 1: When Notifications Are Required {#when-required}

## Subsection 1: Mandatory Notification Scenarios {#mandatory-scenarios}

**Notifications MUST be implemented for:**

1. **AI/OpenAI API Calls**: Any call to OpenAI API (GPT-4, GPT-3.5, etc.)
2. **Long-Running Operations**: Any operation expected to exceed 2 seconds
3. **Multi-Stage Processes**: Operations with multiple sequential steps
4. **Filter-Triggered Regeneration**: When filter changes trigger AI content regeneration
5. **Async Processing**: Operations using `later::later()` or similar async patterns
6. **Batch Processing**: Operations processing multiple items sequentially

## Subsection 2: Process Duration Thresholds {#duration-thresholds}

| Duration | Notification Requirement | Notification Type |
|----------|-------------------------|-------------------|
| < 2 seconds | Optional | Simple spinner/loading |
| 2-10 seconds | Required | Initial notification |
| > 10 seconds | **Mandatory** | Multi-stage progressive notifications |
| > 30 seconds | **Mandatory** | Multi-stage with ETA/progress percentage |

## Subsection 3: Exemption Cases {#exemptions}

**Notifications may be omitted for:**

1. **Instant Operations**: Operations consistently completing in < 1 second
2. **Background Sync**: Silent background synchronization (with status indicator elsewhere)
3. **Real-Time Streaming**: When UI updates in real-time via streaming API

# Section 2: Notification Content Requirements {#content-requirements}

## Subsection 1: Language Requirements {#language-requirements}

**Per UI_R007 (UI Text Standardization):**

1. **Traditional Chinese UI**: Use Traditional Chinese for all notifications
2. **English UI**: Use English for all notifications
3. **No Language Mixing**: Never mix English in Chinese UI or vice versa
4. **Translation Required**: All notification text must go through translation system

```r
# ✅ CORRECT: Traditional Chinese for Chinese UI
showNotification(
  "📊 正在提取資料...",
  id = "process_stage",
  duration = NULL,
  type = "default"
)

# ❌ INCORRECT: English in Chinese UI
showNotification(
  "Extracting data...",  # WRONG - violates UI_R007
  id = "process_stage",
  duration = NULL
)
```

## Subsection 2: Emoji Usage {#emoji-usage}

**Required emoji patterns for visual clarity:**

| Process Type | Emoji | Example Usage |
|--------------|-------|---------------|
| Data Processing | 📊 | "📊 正在提取資料..." |
| Analysis/Calculation | 🔍 | "🔍 正在執行分析..." |
| AI Naming/Labeling | 🏷️ | "🏷️ 正在產生分群名稱..." |
| AI Report/Text | 📝 | "📝 正在產生AI報告..." |
| Clustering/Grouping | 🎯 | "🎯 正在執行分群..." |
| Completion | ✅ | "✅ 完成！" |
| Error | ❌ | "❌ 處理失敗" |

```r
# Standard emoji patterns
STAGE_MESSAGES <- list(
  data_extract = "📊 正在提取資料...",
  clustering = "🎯 正在執行分群...",
  ai_naming = "🏷️ 正在產生分群名稱...",
  ai_report = "📝 正在產生AI報告...",
  complete = "✅ 完成！"
)
```

## Subsection 3: Message Structure {#message-structure}

**Required notification message format:**

```
[Emoji] [Present Progressive Verb] [Object] [Optional Context]
```

**Examples:**
- "📊 正在提取資料..." (Data extraction)
- "🎯 正在執行分群 (K=5)..." (Clustering with context)
- "🏷️ 正在產生分群名稱 (2/5 已完成)..." (Progress context)
- "📝 正在產生AI報告..." (AI report generation)

# Section 3: Implementation Patterns {#implementation-patterns}

## Subsection 1: Single-Stage Notification {#single-stage-notification}

**For operations with one main stage (2-10 seconds):**

```r
#' Single-stage AI operation with notification
#'
#' @param input Shiny input object
#' @param ai_prompt Prompt to send to OpenAI
#' @return AI response
ai_single_stage_operation <- function(input, ai_prompt) {

  # Initial notification
  notification_id <- showNotification(
    "🤖 正在呼叫AI...",
    duration = NULL,  # Persist until removed
    type = "default",
    id = "ai_process"
  )

  tryCatch({
    # AI operation
    result <- call_openai_api(ai_prompt)

    # Completion notification
    removeNotification("ai_process")
    showNotification(
      "✅ AI 處理完成！",
      duration = 3,  # Auto-dismiss after 3 seconds
      type = "message"
    )

    return(result)

  }, error = function(e) {
    # Error notification
    removeNotification("ai_process")
    showNotification(
      paste0("❌ AI 處理失敗: ", e$message),
      duration = 5,
      type = "error"
    )
    return(NULL)
  })
}
```

## Subsection 2: Multi-Stage Progressive Notification {#multi-stage-notification}

**For operations with multiple sequential stages (> 10 seconds):**

```r
#' Multi-stage AI analysis with progressive notifications
#'
#' @param session Shiny session object
#' @param filtered_data Reactive data source
#' @param k_clusters Number of clusters
#' @return Analysis results
ai_multi_stage_analysis <- function(session, filtered_data, k_clusters) {

  # Define stage messages
  STAGE_MESSAGES <- list(
    stage1_data = "📊 正在提取資料...",
    stage2_cluster = paste0("🎯 正在執行分群 (K=", k_clusters, ")..."),
    stage3_naming = "🏷️ 正在產生分群名稱...",
    stage4_report = "📝 正在產生AI報告...",
    complete = "✅ 分析完成！"
  )

  # Stage 1: Data Extraction
  showNotification(
    STAGE_MESSAGES$stage1_data,
    id = "ai_progress",
    duration = NULL,
    type = "default"
  )

  tryCatch({
    # Extract data
    data <- filtered_data()
    Sys.sleep(1)  # Simulate processing

    # Stage 2: Clustering
    removeNotification("ai_progress")
    showNotification(
      STAGE_MESSAGES$stage2_cluster,
      id = "ai_progress",
      duration = NULL,
      type = "default"
    )

    # Perform clustering
    cluster_result <- perform_clustering(data, k_clusters)
    Sys.sleep(2)  # Simulate processing

    # Stage 3: AI Naming
    removeNotification("ai_progress")
    showNotification(
      STAGE_MESSAGES$stage3_naming,
      id = "ai_progress",
      duration = NULL,
      type = "default"
    )

    # Generate AI names
    cluster_names <- generate_ai_cluster_names(cluster_result)
    Sys.sleep(3)  # AI API call

    # Stage 4: AI Report Generation
    removeNotification("ai_progress")
    showNotification(
      STAGE_MESSAGES$stage4_report,
      id = "ai_progress",
      duration = NULL,
      type = "default"
    )

    # Generate AI report
    ai_report <- generate_ai_report(cluster_result, cluster_names)
    Sys.sleep(5)  # AI API call

    # Completion
    removeNotification("ai_progress")
    showNotification(
      STAGE_MESSAGES$complete,
      duration = 3,
      type = "message"
    )

    return(list(
      clusters = cluster_result,
      names = cluster_names,
      report = ai_report
    ))

  }, error = function(e) {
    # Error handling
    removeNotification("ai_progress")
    showNotification(
      paste0("❌ 分析失敗: ", e$message),
      duration = 5,
      type = "error"
    )
    return(NULL)
  })
}
```

## Subsection 3: Filter Change Handler Pattern {#filter-change-handler}

**Pattern for filter changes that trigger AI regeneration:**

```r
#' Filter change handler with regeneration notification
#'
#' @description Proper notification when filters change and AI must regenerate
observeEvent(c(input$filter1, input$filter2, input$filter3), {

  # Clear previous AI results
  ai_results$clusters <- NULL
  ai_results$names <- NULL
  ai_results$report <- NULL

  # Notify user of regeneration requirement
  showNotification(
    "🔄 篩選條件已變更。AI 分析將重新產生...",
    duration = 3,
    type = "warning"
  )

  # Schedule async regeneration with later::later()
  later::later(function() {
    # Run multi-stage analysis with progressive notifications
    new_results <- ai_multi_stage_analysis(
      session = session,
      filtered_data = reactive({ get_filtered_data() }),
      k_clusters = input$k_clusters
    )

    # Update reactive values
    ai_results$clusters <- new_results$clusters
    ai_results$names <- new_results$names
    ai_results$report <- new_results$report
  }, delay = 0.5)  # Small delay to allow UI update
}, ignoreInit = TRUE, ignoreNULL = FALSE)
```

# Section 4: UX Psychology Rationale {#ux-psychology}

## Subsection 1: Static Message Problem {#static-message-problem}

**User Feedback Evidence:**

> "我覺得『Filters changed. AI analysis will be regenerated...』光是這個很難讓人覺得要等而會感覺好向壞掉了"
>
> Translation: "Just seeing 'Filters changed. AI analysis will be regenerated...' doesn't make people feel like they should wait; instead it feels like the system is broken."

**Psychological Issues with Static Messages:**

1. **No Progress Indication**: User cannot tell if system is working or frozen
2. **Uncertainty Anxiety**: Unknown duration creates stress and frustration
3. **Broken System Perception**: After 10 seconds, users assume error occurred
4. **Loss of Trust**: Repeated uncertain waits reduce confidence in system

## Subsection 2: Progressive Notification Benefits {#progressive-benefits}

**Psychological Benefits (Nielsen Norman Group Research):**

1. **Reduced Perceived Wait Time**:
   - Progressive feedback reduces perceived wait by up to 40%
   - Users tolerate longer waits when they understand progress

2. **Increased Trust**:
   - Transparent stages demonstrate system competence
   - Users feel "in the loop" about what's happening

3. **Reduced Anxiety**:
   - Known stages replace uncertainty with expectations
   - Users can plan whether to wait or multitask

4. **Professional Perception**:
   - Well-designed feedback indicates quality software
   - Users perceive faster, more reliable system

## Subsection 3: Stage Notification Psychology {#stage-psychology}

**Why multi-stage notifications are superior:**

```
Static Notification (BAD):
[0s]  "AI analysis will be regenerated..."
[10s] ... still same message ... (user thinks: broken?)
[20s] ... still same message ... (user thinks: definitely broken)
[30s] Done (user thinks: that was painful)

Progressive Notification (GOOD):
[0s]  "📊 正在提取資料..."
[2s]  "🎯 正在執行分群..." (user thinks: oh, it's working on clustering now)
[8s]  "🏷️ 正在產生分群名稱..." (user thinks: makes sense, naming next)
[15s] "📝 正在產生AI報告..." (user thinks: almost done!)
[25s] "✅ 完成！" (user thinks: that was fast and professional)
```

**Key Difference**: Same 25-second duration, dramatically different user experience.

# Section 5: Technical Implementation Details {#technical-implementation}

## Subsection 1: Notification ID Management {#notification-id-management}

**Use consistent notification IDs for stage progression:**

```r
# ✅ CORRECT: Reuse same ID for stage progression
showNotification("Stage 1...", id = "process_progress", duration = NULL)
removeNotification("process_progress")
showNotification("Stage 2...", id = "process_progress", duration = NULL)
removeNotification("process_progress")
showNotification("Complete!", duration = 3)

# ❌ INCORRECT: Creating multiple notifications
showNotification("Stage 1...", duration = NULL)  # No ID - can't remove
showNotification("Stage 2...", duration = NULL)  # Stacks on top
# Result: Notification stack-up, UI clutter
```

## Subsection 2: Duration Parameters {#duration-parameters}

**Proper use of duration parameter:**

| Notification Type | Duration | Reason |
|-------------------|----------|--------|
| In-Progress Stage | `NULL` | Must persist until next stage |
| Completion Message | `3` | Auto-dismiss after 3 seconds |
| Error Message | `5` | Allow time to read error details |
| Warning Message | `4` | Balance visibility and auto-dismiss |

```r
# Progress notification - must persist
showNotification("Processing...", duration = NULL)  # ✅ Correct

# Completion - auto-dismiss
showNotification("Done!", duration = 3)  # ✅ Correct

# Error - allow reading time
showNotification("Error: ...", duration = 5)  # ✅ Correct
```

## Subsection 3: Integration with later::later() {#later-integration}

**Proper async processing with notifications:**

```r
#' Async AI processing with notifications
#'
#' @param trigger Reactive trigger
observeEvent(trigger(), {

  # Initial notification
  showNotification(
    "🔄 開始處理...",
    id = "async_process",
    duration = NULL
  )

  # Schedule async work
  later::later(function() {

    tryCatch({
      # Stage 1
      removeNotification("async_process")
      showNotification("📊 正在提取資料...", id = "async_process", duration = NULL)
      data <- extract_data()

      # Stage 2
      removeNotification("async_process")
      showNotification("🤖 正在呼叫AI...", id = "async_process", duration = NULL)
      result <- call_ai_api(data)

      # Completion
      removeNotification("async_process")
      showNotification("✅ 完成！", duration = 3, type = "message")

      # Update reactive values
      ai_result$value <- result

    }, error = function(e) {
      removeNotification("async_process")
      showNotification(
        paste0("❌ 處理失敗: ", e$message),
        duration = 5,
        type = "error"
      )
    })

  }, delay = 0.1)  # Small delay to ensure notification shows
})
```

# Section 6: Compliance Requirements {#compliance-requirements}

## Subsection 1: Component Audit Checklist {#component-audit}

**All components must be audited for compliance with UI_R019:**

- [ ] **OpenAI API Calls**: Do all OpenAI API calls have notifications?
- [ ] **Long Operations**: Do operations > 2s have initial notifications?
- [ ] **Multi-Stage**: Do operations > 10s have progressive stage notifications?
- [ ] **Filter Changes**: Do filter changes that trigger AI regeneration notify users?
- [ ] **Async Processing**: Do `later::later()` operations have notifications?
- [ ] **Language Compliance**: Are all notifications in correct language (UI_R007)?
- [ ] **Emoji Usage**: Do notifications use appropriate emoji for process type?
- [ ] **Error Handling**: Do errors remove progress notifications and show error messages?

## Subsection 2: Required Components for Audit {#required-components-audit}

**Components that MUST implement UI_R019:**

1. **Market Segmentation Analysis** (`marketSegmentAnalysis/`)
   - Clustering operations
   - AI cluster naming
   - AI segment reports

2. **Competitive Analysis** (`competitiveAnalysis/`)
   - Multi-brand comparison
   - AI strategic insights

3. **Product Positioning** (`productPositioning/`)
   - Positioning calculations
   - AI recommendations

4. **Customer DNA** (`customerDNA/`)
   - Segment generation
   - AI persona creation

5. **Brand Health** (`brandHealth/`)
   - Health score calculations
   - AI diagnostic reports

6. **Trend Analysis** (`trendAnalysis/`)
   - Time series analysis
   - AI trend interpretations

## Subsection 3: New Component Requirements {#new-component-requirements}

**All NEW components must:**

1. **Design Phase**: Include notification flow in component design
2. **Implementation**: Implement progressive notifications from start
3. **Testing**: Test notification timing and user experience
4. **Documentation**: Document notification stages in component README
5. **Code Review**: Verify notification compliance before merge

# Section 7: Testing and Validation {#testing-validation}

## Subsection 1: Manual Testing Procedure {#manual-testing}

**Test procedure for notification compliance:**

```r
# Test Script: UI_R019 Notification Compliance
test_notification_compliance <- function(component_name) {

  cat("Testing UI_R019 Compliance for:", component_name, "\n")

  # Test 1: Initial notification appears
  cat("1. Triggering AI process...\n")
  # Manually trigger process
  # ✅ PASS: Notification appears within 500ms
  # ❌ FAIL: No notification or delayed > 1s

  # Test 2: Progressive stage updates
  cat("2. Monitoring stage progression...\n")
  # Observe notification changes
  # ✅ PASS: Notifications update at each stage
  # ❌ FAIL: Static notification or missing stages

  # Test 3: Timing validation
  cat("3. Validating timing thresholds...\n")
  # Measure operation duration
  # ✅ PASS: Operations > 2s have notifications
  # ❌ FAIL: Long operations without notifications

  # Test 4: Language compliance
  cat("4. Checking language consistency...\n")
  # Verify notification language matches UI language
  # ✅ PASS: All notifications in correct language
  # ❌ FAIL: Language mixing (English in Chinese UI)

  # Test 5: Completion handling
  cat("5. Verifying completion notification...\n")
  # Wait for process completion
  # ✅ PASS: Completion notification shows and auto-dismisses
  # ❌ FAIL: Progress notification persists or no completion message

  # Test 6: Error handling
  cat("6. Testing error scenario...\n")
  # Trigger error condition
  # ✅ PASS: Error notification shows with details
  # ❌ FAIL: Progress notification persists or no error message
}
```

## Subsection 2: Automated Testing {#automated-testing}

**Automated test pattern for notification presence:**

```r
#' Test notification implementation
#'
#' @param component Component to test
#' @param test_data Test data
test_that("UI_R019: AI process has progressive notifications", {

  # Setup test environment
  testServer(componentServer, {

    # Trigger AI process
    session$setInputs(trigger_ai = TRUE)

    # Test 1: Initial notification
    expect_true(
      notification_exists("ai_progress"),
      "Initial notification should appear"
    )

    # Test 2: First stage notification
    Sys.sleep(0.5)
    expect_match(
      get_notification_text("ai_progress"),
      "正在提取資料|Extracting data",
      "First stage notification should show data extraction"
    )

    # Test 3: Stage progression
    Sys.sleep(2)
    expect_match(
      get_notification_text("ai_progress"),
      "正在執行分群|Clustering|正在呼叫AI|Calling AI",
      "Notification should update to next stage"
    )

    # Test 4: Completion
    session$flushReact()
    Sys.sleep(5)
    expect_match(
      get_notification_text(),
      "完成|Complete|✅",
      "Completion notification should appear"
    )
  })
})
```

# Section 8: Examples and Anti-Patterns {#examples-anti-patterns}

## Subsection 1: Complete Good Example {#good-example}

**Market Segmentation Analysis component (COMPLIANT):**

```r
#' Market Segmentation Analysis Server
#'
#' @description Implements UI_R019 compliant AI notifications
marketSegmentAnalysisServer <- function(id, filtered_data) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {

    # Reactive values for AI results
    ai_results <- reactiveValues(
      clusters = NULL,
      names = NULL,
      report = NULL
    )

    # Filter change handler with regeneration notification
    observeEvent(c(input$date_range, input$product_filter), {

      # Clear previous results
      ai_results$clusters <- NULL
      ai_results$names <- NULL
      ai_results$report <- NULL

      # Notify user
      showNotification(
        "🔄 篩選條件已變更。AI 分析將重新產生...",
        duration = 3,
        type = "warning"
      )

      # Schedule async regeneration
      later::later(function() {

        # Stage messages
        STAGE_MESSAGES <- list(
          data = "📊 正在提取資料...",
          cluster = paste0("🎯 正在執行分群 (K=", input$k_clusters, ")..."),
          naming = "🏷️ 正在產生分群名稱...",
          report = "📝 正在產生AI報告...",
          complete = "✅ 分析完成！"
        )

        tryCatch({
          # Stage 1: Data
          showNotification(STAGE_MESSAGES$data, id = "ai_progress", duration = NULL)
          data <- filtered_data()

          # Stage 2: Clustering
          removeNotification("ai_progress")
          showNotification(STAGE_MESSAGES$cluster, id = "ai_progress", duration = NULL)
          clusters <- perform_clustering(data, input$k_clusters)

          # Stage 3: AI Naming
          removeNotification("ai_progress")
          showNotification(STAGE_MESSAGES$naming, id = "ai_progress", duration = NULL)
          names <- generate_ai_cluster_names(clusters)

          # Stage 4: AI Report
          removeNotification("ai_progress")
          showNotification(STAGE_MESSAGES$report, id = "ai_progress", duration = NULL)
          report <- generate_ai_report(clusters, names)

          # Update results
          ai_results$clusters <- clusters
          ai_results$names <- names
          ai_results$report <- report

          # Completion
          removeNotification("ai_progress")
          showNotification(STAGE_MESSAGES$complete, duration = 3, type = "message")

        }, error = function(e) {
          removeNotification("ai_progress")
          showNotification(
            paste0("❌ 分析失敗: ", e$message),
            duration = 5,
            type = "error"
          )
        })
      }, delay = 0.5)
    }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

    # Output: AI report
    output$ai_report <- renderText({
      req(ai_results$report)
      ai_results$report
    })
  })
}
```

## Subsection 2: Anti-Pattern Examples {#anti-patterns}

### Anti-Pattern 1: No Notification (VIOLATION)

```r
# ❌ VIOLATION: Long AI operation with no notification
observeEvent(input$generate_report, {

  # Silent processing - user sees nothing
  data <- get_data()
  result <- call_openai_api(data)  # Takes 15 seconds

  # Update output
  output$report <- renderText(result)
})
```

**Problems:**
1. No indication system is working
2. User assumes system is broken after 10 seconds
3. Violates UI_R019 core requirement

### Anti-Pattern 2: Static Message Only (VIOLATION)

```r
# ❌ VIOLATION: Static notification for long process
observeEvent(input$analyze, {

  showNotification("Processing...", duration = NULL)  # Static message

  # Long multi-stage process
  data <- extract_data()  # 3 seconds
  clusters <- cluster_data(data)  # 5 seconds
  names <- ai_generate_names(clusters)  # 10 seconds
  report <- ai_generate_report(clusters, names)  # 15 seconds

  removeNotification()
  showNotification("Done!", duration = 3)
})
```

**Problems:**
1. User sees "Processing..." for 33 seconds
2. No indication of progress through stages
3. Violates progressive notification requirement

### Anti-Pattern 3: English in Chinese UI (VIOLATION)

```r
# ❌ VIOLATION: Language mixing (violates UI_R007)
observeEvent(input$trigger, {

  # Chinese UI but English notification
  showNotification(
    "Generating AI report...",  # WRONG - violates UI_R007
    duration = NULL
  )

  result <- generate_ai_report()

  showNotification("Complete!", duration = 3)  # WRONG
})
```

**Problems:**
1. Violates UI_R007 language standardization
2. Inconsistent user experience
3. Unprofessional appearance

### Anti-Pattern 4: Notification ID Mismanagement (VIOLATION)

```r
# ❌ VIOLATION: Not removing previous notifications
observeEvent(input$process, {

  # Stage 1
  showNotification("Stage 1...", duration = NULL)  # No ID!
  process_stage_1()

  # Stage 2
  showNotification("Stage 2...", duration = NULL)  # No ID!
  process_stage_2()

  # Stage 3
  showNotification("Stage 3...", duration = NULL)  # No ID!
  process_stage_3()

  # Result: 3 stacked notifications
})
```

**Problems:**
1. Notifications stack up instead of replacing
2. UI becomes cluttered
3. User confusion about current stage

# Section 9: Relationship to Other Principles {#relationship-to-other-principles}

## Subsection 1: Relation to MP099 (Real-Time Progress Reporting) {#relation-to-mp099}

UI_R019 is the **UI-specific implementation** of MP099:

- **MP099**: General principle for all progress reporting (console, logs, monitoring)
- **UI_R019**: Specific requirements for Shiny UI notifications

**Integration pattern:**

```r
# Backend follows MP099
message("MAIN: Starting AI analysis...")  # Console progress
message("  Step 1/4: Extracting data...")
data <- extract_data()

# Frontend follows UI_R019
showNotification("📊 正在提取資料...", id = "progress", duration = NULL)  # UI notification
```

## Subsection 2: Relation to UI_R007 (UI Text Standardization) {#relation-to-ui-r007}

UI_R019 **depends on** UI_R007:

- All notification text must follow UI_R007 language requirements
- Notifications must use `translate()` function for internationalization
- No language mixing allowed

```r
# ✅ COMPLIANT with both UI_R019 and UI_R007
showNotification(
  translate("Generating AI report..."),  # Uses UI_R007 translation
  id = "ai_progress",  # Follows UI_R019 ID management
  duration = NULL
)
```

## Subsection 3: Relation to MP052 (Unidirectional Data Flow) {#relation-to-mp052}

UI_R019 notifications should **not modify data flow**:

- Notifications are **informational only**
- Do not use notifications to trigger reactive updates
- Notifications follow data changes, not drive them

```r
# ✅ CORRECT: Notification follows data update
observeEvent(input$filter, {
  # Data flow
  new_data <- filter_data(input$filter)
  ai_result$value <- new_data

  # Notification follows (informational only)
  showNotification("Data updated", duration = 2)
})

# ❌ INCORRECT: Using notification to trigger updates
observeEvent(show_notification_trigger(), {
  # WRONG - notification should not drive data flow
  update_data()
})
```

## Subsection 4: Relation to UI_R001 (UI-Server-Defaults Triple) {#relation-to-ui-r001}

Notification logic belongs in **Server component**:

```
Component/
├── componentUI.R         # No notification logic
├── componentServer.R     # ✅ Notification logic here
└── componentDefaults.R   # No notification logic
```

# Section 10: Migration Guide for Existing Components {#migration-guide}

## Subsection 1: Identifying Non-Compliant Components {#identifying-non-compliant}

**Search patterns to find violations:**

```bash
# Find components with AI calls but potentially no notifications
grep -r "openai" scripts/global_scripts/10_rshinyapp_components/ | \
  grep -v "showNotification"

# Find long operations without notifications
grep -r "Sys.sleep" scripts/global_scripts/10_rshinyapp_components/ | \
  grep -v "showNotification"

# Find filter change handlers without notifications
grep -r "observeEvent.*input.*filter" scripts/global_scripts/10_rshinyapp_components/ | \
  grep -v "showNotification"
```

## Subsection 2: Step-by-Step Migration {#step-by-step-migration}

**Migration process for existing components:**

### Step 1: Identify AI/Long Operations

```r
# Find all OpenAI API calls and long operations
# Mark each operation's expected duration
# Categorize: < 2s, 2-10s, > 10s
```

### Step 2: Define Stage Messages

```r
# Create stage message list
STAGE_MESSAGES <- list(
  stage1 = "📊 正在提取資料...",
  stage2 = "🎯 正在執行分群...",
  stage3 = "🏷️ 正在產生名稱...",
  complete = "✅ 完成！"
)
```

### Step 3: Add Initial Notification

```r
# Before first operation
showNotification(
  STAGE_MESSAGES$stage1,
  id = "process_progress",
  duration = NULL
)
```

### Step 4: Add Stage Progressions

```r
# Before each subsequent stage
removeNotification("process_progress")
showNotification(
  STAGE_MESSAGES$stage2,
  id = "process_progress",
  duration = NULL
)
```

### Step 5: Add Completion/Error Handling

```r
# Wrap in tryCatch
tryCatch({
  # ... processing ...

  removeNotification("process_progress")
  showNotification(STAGE_MESSAGES$complete, duration = 3)

}, error = function(e) {
  removeNotification("process_progress")
  showNotification(
    paste0("❌ 處理失敗: ", e$message),
    duration = 5,
    type = "error"
  )
})
```

### Step 6: Test User Experience

```r
# Manual testing checklist:
# [ ] Notification appears immediately (< 500ms)
# [ ] Each stage shows correct message
# [ ] Completion auto-dismisses
# [ ] Error handling works
# [ ] No notification stacking
# [ ] Correct language (no mixing)
```

## Subsection 3: Priority Migration List {#priority-migration}

**Recommended migration order (highest impact first):**

1. **Market Segmentation Analysis** - Most visible, longest duration
2. **Competitive Analysis** - Multiple AI calls, user expects feedback
3. **Customer DNA** - AI persona generation, long wait times
4. **Product Positioning** - Strategic analysis, professional appearance important
5. **Trend Analysis** - Time series calculations, ETA helpful
6. **Brand Health** - Diagnostic reports, user anxiety high

# Section 11: Performance Considerations {#performance-considerations}

## Subsection 1: Notification Overhead {#notification-overhead}

**Performance impact of notifications:**

- **Minimal**: Each `showNotification()` call adds < 10ms
- **Network**: No network calls, client-side only
- **Memory**: Negligible (< 1KB per notification)
- **Recommended Maximum**: 10 stage updates per process

```r
# Performance comparison
without_notifications <- system.time({
  result <- long_ai_operation()
})
# Time: 25.3 seconds

with_notifications <- system.time({
  showNotification("Processing...", id = "progress", duration = NULL)
  result <- long_ai_operation()
  removeNotification("progress")
  showNotification("Done!", duration = 3)
})
# Time: 25.32 seconds (< 1% overhead)
```

## Subsection 2: Notification Frequency Guidelines {#notification-frequency}

**Best practices for notification frequency:**

| Process Duration | Recommended Stages | Update Frequency |
|------------------|-------------------|------------------|
| 2-5 seconds | 1-2 stages | Initial + completion |
| 5-15 seconds | 2-3 stages | Every 3-5 seconds |
| 15-30 seconds | 3-5 stages | Every 5-7 seconds |
| > 30 seconds | 4-6 stages + ETA | Every 7-10 seconds |

```r
# ✅ GOOD: Appropriate stage granularity (15s process)
Stage 1: Data extraction (0-3s)
Stage 2: Clustering (3-8s)
Stage 3: AI naming (8-15s)
Completion (15s)

# ❌ BAD: Too many stages (15s process)
Stage 1: Initialize (0-1s)
Stage 2: Load data (1-2s)
Stage 3: Validate data (2-3s)
Stage 4: Transform data (3-5s)
Stage 5: Cluster (5-8s)
Stage 6: Validate clusters (8-9s)
Stage 7: Call AI (9-12s)
Stage 8: Process AI (12-14s)
Stage 9: Validate AI (14-15s)
Completion (15s)
# User overwhelmed with updates
```

# Section 12: Accessibility Considerations {#accessibility}

## Subsection 1: Screen Reader Compatibility {#screen-reader-compatibility}

**Ensure notifications are accessible:**

```r
# ✅ ACCESSIBLE: Use ARIA live regions
showNotification(
  "📊 正在提取資料...",
  id = "progress",
  duration = NULL,
  type = "default"
)
# Shiny automatically creates ARIA live region
# Screen readers announce notification content

# Additional accessibility: Provide text alternative
output$progress_text <- renderText({
  req(ai_processing())
  current_stage()  # Text description of current stage
})
```

## Subsection 2: Visual Alternatives {#visual-alternatives}

**Complement notifications with visual progress indicators:**

```r
# Progress bar alongside notifications
output$progress_bar <- renderUI({
  req(ai_processing())

  progress_percent <- calculate_progress()

  div(
    class = "progress",
    div(
      class = "progress-bar",
      role = "progressbar",
      style = paste0("width: ", progress_percent, "%"),
      `aria-valuenow` = progress_percent,
      `aria-valuemin` = "0",
      `aria-valuemax` = "100",
      paste0(progress_percent, "%")
    )
  )
})
```

# Section 13: Internationalization {#internationalization}

## Subsection 1: Multi-Language Support {#multi-language-support}

**Proper internationalization of notifications:**

```r
# Define stage messages in translation dictionary
ui_terminology_dict <- data.frame(
  English = c(
    "Extracting data...",
    "Performing clustering...",
    "Generating AI names...",
    "Generating AI report...",
    "Complete!"
  ),
  Chinese = c(
    "正在提取資料...",
    "正在執行分群...",
    "正在產生分群名稱...",
    "正在產生AI報告...",
    "完成！"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Use translate() function per UI_R007
showNotification(
  translate("Extracting data..."),  # Automatically translates based on app language
  id = "progress",
  duration = NULL
)
```

## Subsection 2: Emoji Consistency Across Languages {#emoji-consistency}

**Emoji usage is language-agnostic:**

```r
# ✅ GOOD: Same emoji across languages
English: "📊 Extracting data..."
Chinese: "📊 正在提取資料..."

# Emoji provides visual consistency across languages
# Users recognize process type regardless of language
```

# Section 14: Future Enhancements {#future-enhancements}

## Subsection 1: Planned Improvements {#planned-improvements}

**Potential future enhancements to UI_R019:**

1. **Progress Percentage**: Add percentage completion to notifications
   ```r
   showNotification(
     "📊 正在提取資料... (20%)",
     id = "progress",
     duration = NULL
   )
   ```

2. **ETA Estimates**: Calculate and display estimated time remaining
   ```r
   showNotification(
     "🏷️ 正在產生分群名稱... (約 15 秒)",
     id = "progress",
     duration = NULL
   )
   ```

3. **Cancellation Support**: Allow users to cancel long operations
   ```r
   showNotification(
     tagList(
       "📝 正在產生AI報告...",
       actionButton("cancel_ai", "取消", size = "xs")
     ),
     id = "progress",
     duration = NULL
   )
   ```

4. **Notification Sound**: Optional audio feedback for completion
   ```r
   showNotification(
     "✅ 完成！",
     duration = 3,
     type = "message",
     sound = TRUE  # Play completion sound
   )
   ```

## Subsection 2: Research Opportunities {#research-opportunities}

**Areas for UX research:**

1. **Optimal Stage Granularity**: Study ideal number of stages for different durations
2. **Emoji Effectiveness**: Measure impact of emoji on user comprehension
3. **Duration Thresholds**: Validate 2-second and 10-second thresholds with user testing
4. **Notification Fatigue**: Study when users become annoyed by too many updates

---

**Implementation Priority**: HIGH - Critical for AI-powered components
**Validation Required**: All components using OpenAI API or async processing
**User Impact**: HIGH - Directly affects perceived system quality and trust
**Related Principles**: MP099, UI_R007, MP052, UI_R001

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 19*
*User Feedback: "光是這個很難讓人覺得要等而會感覺好向壞掉了" (2025-11-02)*
*Research Foundation: Nielsen Norman Group - Progress Indicators*
